name: F.I.R.S.T. Principles for Tests
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 9: Unit Tests
problem: Tests that are slow, dependent on external state, non-repeatable, or hard to read are skipped, flaky, or ignored over time, destroying the value of the test suite as a safety net.
steps: Fast: Tests should run in milliseconds; mock any I/O, database, or network dependency.
Independent: No test should depend on the output or state of another; tests must run in any order.
Repeatable: Tests must produce the same result in any environment (local, CI, production-like).
Self-validating: Every test must return a clear boolean pass/fail with no manual log inspection.
Timely: Write tests just before the production code they validate, not months after.
